Commissioll

322·5660 
Honorable J. Stephen Peace 
Member of the Assembly 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Assemblyman Peace: 
Executive/Legal 
32~·5901 
Enforcement 
322·6441 
October 18, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-83-232 
You have asked whether you will have any filing obligations 
as a result of the provision of one day's free room and board to 
your secretary in connection with a tour of a congregate care 
facility in your district. 
You only have to report gifts which 
you personally receive on your Statement of Economic Interests. 
You do not have to report a gift to a member of your staff, even 
if the gift is made in connection with the staff member's 
legislative business in your district. 
If the staff member is required to file a Statement of 
Economic Interests under the Assembly's Conflict of Interest 
Code, however, the staff member may have a reporting 
obligation. 
Therefore, if your secretary has to file a 
Statement of Economic Interests, she should contact our 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division for advice on what to 
report. 
If she does not have to file a Statement of Economic 
Interests, no reporting will be required at all. 
I hope this answers your questions. 
BAM:plh 
Very truly yours, 
dLJ-d.),J_1; JJte-.[ v---
Barbara A. Milman 
Chief 
Legal Division

ASSEMBLYMAN, EIGHTIETH DISTRICT 
October 14, 1983 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Barbara Milman -
Legal Division 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Ms. Milman: 
Vice-Chairman 
AGING AND LONG TERM CARE 
COMMITTEES: 
Agriculture 
Water, Parks and Wildlife 
Commission of the Californias 
I would like clarification on an lssue regarding my secretary, 
Mary Kurbatoff, of my Capitol staff. 
This last session, I introduced AB 2025 dealing with congre-
gate care facilities. 
Mrs. Kurbatoff will be in my district on 
legislative business during the interim and has been offered 
free room and board for one day as well as a tour of a congregate 
care facility within the district. 
My question is by her accepting this gratuity, is it neces-
sary for me to report this on my next filing? 
Your expeditious 
response would be most appreciated. 
SP:k

Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to me. 
If you have any 
questions about your advice request, please contact me 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. 
BAM:km

Very truly yours,

sections.conclusion: You only have to report gifts which you personally receive on your Statement of Economic Interests. You do not have to report a gift to a member of your staff, even if the gift is made in connection with the staff member's legislative business in your district. If the staff member is required to file a Statement of Economic Interests under the Assembly's Conflict of Interest Code, however, the staff member may have a reporting obligation.
sections.question_synthetic: Must an Assembly member report a gift of room and board provided to their staff secretary in connection with legislative business, specifically a tour of a congregate care facility in the district?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Assembly member is not required to report the gift to their secretary on their Statement of Economic Interests. However, if the secretary is required to file a Statement of Economic Interests, the secretary themselves may have a reporting obligation and should seek technical assistance.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Complete advice letter with clear Q&C sections. Document includes both the incoming request letter and FPPC response letter. OCR quality is good with minor notation artifacts. Letter No. A-83-232 from October 18, 1983.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Assembly Member Peace that gifts provided to staff members do not require reporting by the legislator on their Statement of Economic Interests, but the staff member may have independent reporting obligations if they are required to file their own statement.
